title: Kanbus clock drops notifications under burst
description:
Observed:
- When many events arrive quickly, only a subset appear on the clock.

Expected:
- All events are shown, even if delayed.

Planned fix:
- Remove drop-on-full behavior; backpressure on the auto-notice queue.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Starting fix: replace auto-notice drop-on-full with backpressure; add high-water log and async queue test.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Implemented backpressure: auto-notice enqueue now awaits queue.put; removed drop-on-full; added backlog log when qsize>=48. Added async test ensuring put blocks when queue full. Tests: uv run pytest tests/test_kanbus_clock.py -q (38 passed). Ready for visual verification.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Added workspace-root timeout guard: kbs show from workspace uses 2s timeout, disables workspace lookup after first timeout; falls back to project-root to avoid 10s stalls that made notifications appear dropped. Tests: py_compile + queue test.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Updated kbs show lookup: use --project-root when event repo root is known to avoid workspace-root 10s timeouts; workspace lookup now secondary. Added test for --project-root arg. Tests: uv run pytest tests/test_kanbus_clock.py -q (40 passed).

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Fix: project-root lookup now runs with cwd=project_root (not workspace root) while passing --project-root, eliminating 3s timeouts that stalled event handling. Tests: py_compile + kbs show arg test.
